title: Market Brief — Monday, April 20, 2026
summary: Compute is scarce across all GPU classes. H100 availability sits at 24% (77/307), H200 at 21% (45/45 with zero slack), and B200 at a near-severe 17% (136/429). B200 spot dropped 5.1% day-over-day to $3.94/hr despite surging utilization up 32.5pp in 30 days, signaling aggressive new supply hitting a demand wall. H100 bucked the trend, jumping 7% to $2.00/hr as legacy workloads cling to proven silicon.
paragraphs: Compute is scarce across all GPU classes. H100 availability sits at 24% (77/307), H200 at 21% (45/45 with zero slack), and B200 at a near-severe 17% (136/429). B200 spot dropped 5.1% day-over-day to $3.94/hr despite surging utilization up 32.5pp in 30 days, signaling aggressive new supply hitting a demand wall. H100 bucked the trend, jumping 7% to $2.00/hr as legacy workloads cling to proven silicon.

B200 is the only GPU where inference margins work at mid-tier pricing: Llama 70B at medium tier yields 4% margin on B200 versus negative 95% on H100 and negative 106% on H200. TCO-adjusted cost (1.25x spot) puts H200 inference at $7.73/M tokens, meaning anyone selling at medium-tier pricing is losing roughly $4.73/M tokens per call. DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.42/M output is burning cash. Provider grades: OpenAI Neutral, Anthropic Strong, Google Neutral, DeepSeek Burning Cash, xAI Weak.

B200 spot at $3.94/hr is overvalued given 86.8% annualized price volatility and thinning 17% availability that one supply shock could reprice violently. DeepSeek R1 at $2.50/M output on sub-dollar input is the best risk-adjusted inference trade in the stack, offering frontier-adjacent reasoning at commodity pricing before DeepSeek reprices to survive.
bullets: 17% B200 availability (136 of 429 GPUs free) puts the newest accelerator class near severe shortage territory.
21% H200 availability with all 45 tracked units in a single pool means one large reservation could zero out supply overnight.
$2.88/M token cost on B200 undercuts H100 by 51%, making it the only margin-positive platform at mid-tier pricing.
86.8% annualized B200 price volatility is the highest of any GPU class, nearly 5x the H100 rate of 19.2%.
$0.42/M output from DeepSeek V3.2 sits roughly 85% below TCO-adjusted B200 inference cost of $3.60/M.
21.43 on the LLMflation Index means token prices have fallen 79% from the GPT-4 baseline in just three years.
